ShaderNodeSeparateXYZ.description: Split vector into X, Y, Z components

ShaderNodeCombineXYZ.description: Combine X, Y, Z into vector

ShaderNodeSeparateRGB.description: Split color into R, G, B channels

ShaderNodeCombineRGB.description: Combine R, G, B into color

unity_chain.description: Sample 2D texture with UV input; assign texture asset manually

steps.note: Add node via: Add > Texture > Sample Texture 2D
Wire UV output to the UV port
Assign the matching texture asset from the Unity project
Use RGBA for color or A for alpha

unity_chain.blender_conversion_notes: Export texture from Blender via Image > Save As. Import into Unity Assets folder.

unity_chain.description: Sample cubemap texture for environment/reflections

steps.note: Add node via: Add > Texture > Sample Cubemap
Use Reflection Vector node for proper cubemap UVs
Assign exported .hdr or cubemap asset

unity_chain.blender_conversion_notes: Bake environment to cubemap in Blender. Export as .hdr and import into Unity as Cubemap texture type.

unity_chain.description: Map Blender texture coordinate outputs to Unity equivalents

steps.note: Blender TexCoord has multiple outputs. Map each as needed:
Add > Input > UV
Use Transform node in Object space
Add > Input > Normal Vector
Add > Input > Screen Position (approximation)
Same approximation as Camera

unity_chain.blender_conversion_notes: Identify which TexCoord output is actually used in the Blender graph before mapping.

unity_chain.description: Reference a specific named UV channel

steps.note: Add > Input > UV
Set UV channel index matching the Blender UV map (usually channel 0)

unity_chain.blender_conversion_notes: Check Blender UV map name. Match to Unity mesh UV channel (UV0, UV1, etc.).

unity_chain.description: Access custom vertex attribute data

steps.note: If attribute is vertex color: Add > Input > Vertex Color
For other attributes: Create a custom vertex shader or use Vertex ID node

unity_chain.blender_conversion_notes: Identify attribute type in Blender (color, UV, custom). Color attributes map to Vertex Color in Unity.

ShaderNodeFresnel.description: Fresnel effect based on view angle

unity_chain.description: View-dependent Fresnel falloff effect

steps.note: Add > Input > Fresnel
Convert Blender IOR to Unity power: Power ≈ 5 / IOR. IOR 1.5 → Power ~3.3

unity_chain.blender_conversion_notes: Blender uses IOR (Index of Refraction), Unity uses a power value. Use formula: Power ≈ 5/IOR as approximation.

ShaderNodeNormalMap.description: Convert height map to normal map

unity_chain.description: Convert tangent-space normal map texture to world normal

steps.note: Add > Texture > Sample Texture 2D
Set texture type to Normal in Unity importer for correct unpacking
Add > Artistic > Normal From Texture
Match the Strength value from the Blender NormalMap node

unity_chain.blender_conversion_notes: Ensure the normal texture is imported as Normal Map type in Unity (Texture Import Settings > Texture Type > Normal map).

unity_chain.description: Transform UV coordinates using tiling and offset

steps.note: Add > UV > Tiling And Offset
Map Blender Scale X/Y to Tiling
Map Blender Location X/Y to Offset
Blender Rotation is NOT supported by Tiling And Offset. If rotation is non-zero, chain a Rotate node after.
If Blender Mapping has Z rotation: Add Math Rotate node after Tiling And Offset

unity_chain.blender_conversion_notes: Blender Mapping node supports full 3D transforms. Unity Tiling And Offset only handles 2D scale+offset. Use Rotate node for rotated UVs.

ShaderNodeMixRGB.description: Blend between two colors with modes

unity_chain.description: Blend two colors based on blend mode and factor

steps.note: Check the Blender MixRGB blend mode. Default is Mix (Lerp).
For Mix mode: Add > Math > Lerp

modes.SCREEN: Chain: Add(A, B) -> Subtract(Multiply(A,B)) [A + B - A*B]
modes.OVERLAY: Chain: Lerp(2*A*B, 1-2*(1-A)*(1-B), Step(0.5, A)) -- complex chain
modes.DIFFERENCE: Use Subtract node then Abs: Abs(A - B)

unity_chain.blender_conversion_notes: Blender MixRGB has many blend modes. Unity's Lerp covers the basic Mix. Other modes require chained math nodes as described above.

ShaderNodeClamp.description: Clamp value between min and max

ShaderNodeBsdfPrincipled.description: Master shader decomposed into Color, Metallic, Roughness, Normal, Emission

unity_chain.description: Chain 5 Unity nodes: Base Color + Metallic + Smoothness (1-Roughness) + Normal + Emission
Matte/diffuse surface approximated via Base Color in PBR

steps.note: Add a PBR Master node for the output
Route diffuse color to Base Color
No metallic for pure diffuse
No glossiness for pure diffuse

unity_chain.blender_conversion_notes: Blender diffuse is matte. Unity PBR approximation: set Metallic=0, Smoothness=0.

unity_chain.description: Glossy/mirror surface decomposed to metallic+roughness workflow

steps.note: Full metallic for glossy
Add > Math > One Minus
Unity Smoothness = 1 - Blender Roughness

unity_chain.blender_conversion_notes: Blender Glossy uses Roughness. Unity uses Smoothness (inverted). Apply One Minus node on roughness value.

unity_chain.description: Glass shader approximated with transparent + specular combination

steps.note: Set material Surface to Transparent in Unity
Add > Input > Fresnel
Fresnel controls opacity edge
High smoothness for glass-like reflection

unity_chain.blender_conversion_notes: True refractive glass is not real-time. Use transparent surface + Fresnel opacity + high smoothness as approximation.

unity_chain.description: Transparent cutout or alpha blend material

steps.note: In Unity material inspector set Surface Type to Transparent
Connect alpha input to Master node Alpha port
For cutout: set AlphaClip checkbox and connect threshold

unity_chain.blender_conversion_notes: Blender transparent BSDF controls opacity. Set Unity material to Transparent surface and drive Alpha from the Blender Fac input.

unity_chain.description: Self-illuminating emissive surface

steps.note: Multiply emission color by strength value
Enable Emission in the Unity material inspector (Emission checkbox must be ON)

unity_chain.blender_conversion_notes: Blender Emission has Strength. Unity uses color intensity. Multiply the color by the strength scalar before connecting to Emission.

ShaderNodeBump.description: Height map to normal map conversion (approximation)

unity_chain.description: Convert a height/bump map to normal map

steps.note: Add > Artistic > Normal From Height

steps.from: [Height texture output] -> Normal From Height (input: In)

steps.note: Match strength to Blender Bump node Strength input

steps.from: Normal From Height (output: Out) -> Normal (Master node)

unity_chain.blender_conversion_notes: Blender Bump takes height and outputs a normal. Unity Normal From Height does the same. Match the Strength parameter.

unity_chain.description: Procedural noise texture (Perlin / Simplex)

steps.note: Add > Procedural > Simple Noise (or Gradient Noise)
Add > Input > UV
Control noise scale via Tiling And Offset

steps.from: UV -> Tiling And Offset -> Simple Noise (input: UV)

steps.note: For Detail/Roughness blend: chain a second Simple Noise and Add/Lerp the results

unity_chain.blender_conversion_notes: Blender Noise has Scale, Detail, Roughness, Distortion params. Unity Simple Noise has Scale only. Add multiple noise nodes for detail approximation.

unity_chain.description: Voronoi / cellular noise texture

steps.note: Add > Procedural > Voronoi Noise
Map Blender Scale to Cell Density
Blender Voronoi has F1/F2/Smooth modes. Unity Voronoi output is distance-based. For smooth: add Smoothstep after.

unity_chain.blender_conversion_notes: Match Cell Density to Blender Scale. Blender Randomness maps to Unity Voronoi Angle Offset.

unity_chain.description: Procedural gradient from UV position

steps.note: Separate U and V channels
Add > Utility > Sample Gradient for color mapping
For radial gradient: CREATE Length(UV - 0.5) node instead of Split

unity_chain.blender_conversion_notes: Blender gradient types: Linear uses UV.x, Radial uses distance from center, Spherical uses length. Map accordingly in Unity.

unity_chain.description: Procedural wave/stripe pattern

steps.note: UV.x * frequency (Blender Scale)
Add phase offset (Blender Phase Offset)
Add > Math > Sine
Blender Distortion adds extra noise to wave. Chain a Simple Noise and add to UV before Sine for distortion.

unity_chain.blender_conversion_notes: Sine-based wave chain: UV * Scale + Phase -> Sine. Use Smoothstep on output to get clean bands. Add Noise distortion on UV for wavy look.

unity_chain.description: Fractal Brownian Motion (fBm) multi-octave noise

steps.note: FBM = sum of multiple noise layers with increasing frequency and decreasing amplitude
One per octave
Blender Musgrave Detail controls number of octaves (2-16). Unity needs one noise node per octave.

unity_chain.blender_conversion_notes: FBM chain: Sum of (Noise(UV * Lacunarity^i) * Gain^i) for i=0..Detail. Use Lacunarity and Gain from Blender node properties.

unity_chain.description: Procedural brick / tile pattern

steps.note: No direct Unity brick node. Must build from UV math.
Set Tiling to (BrickWidth, BrickHeight)
Add > Math > Fraction (wraps UV per brick cell)
Two Step nodes: one for horizontal edge, one for vertical edge
For offset rows (stagger): add 0.5 to UV.x for every other row using Floor(UV.y) * 0.5 -> Fraction -> Add to UV.x

unity_chain.blender_conversion_notes: Brick pattern: Fraction(UV*scale) -> Step for edges -> Multiply. Add row offset for staggered bricks using Floor(UV.y) * 0.5 mod 1.

unity_chain.description: Procedural checkerboard pattern

steps.note: Set Tiling to checkerboard scale
Add > Math > Floor for UV.x and UV.y
Floor_X + Floor_Y
Fraction(Floor_X + Floor_Y) gives 0.0 or 0.5
Step(0.5, Fraction) gives 0 or 1 alternating

steps.from: Step (Out) -> [output] or Lerp (T) for color blend

unity_chain.blender_conversion_notes: Checker formula: Step(0.5, Fraction(Floor(UV.x) + Floor(UV.y))). This gives a perfect 0/1 checkerboard.

unity_chain.description: Read per-vertex color data from the mesh

steps.note: Add > Input > Vertex Color
Unity uses channel index; match to Blender layer order
Ensure mesh is exported from Blender with Vertex Colors enabled (FBX export > Mesh > Vertex Colors)

unity_chain.blender_conversion_notes: Vertex colors must be exported in FBX. In Blender FBX export: check Mesh > Vertex Colors. Unity auto-detects vertex color stream.

unity_chain.description: Per-vertex tangent space vector

steps.note: Add > Input > Tangent Vector
Ensure mesh has tangents computed. In Blender FBX export > Geometry > Tangent Space = enabled.

unity_chain.blender_conversion_notes: Tangent vectors must be exported. Enable Tangent Space in Blender FBX export settings.

ShaderNodeLayerWeight.description: Blend weight based on view angle or facing

unity_chain.description: View-angle blend weight (Fresnel or Facing)

steps.note: Blend 0.0 = flat, 1.0 = very soft Fresnel. Map: Power = lerp(1, 10, Blend)
For Facing output: use Dot Product of Normal and View Direction, clamped

unity_chain.blender_conversion_notes: LayerWeight outputs Fresnel and Facing. Blender Blend controls sharpness. Approximate with Unity Fresnel + power adjustment.

ShaderNodeMapRange.description: Remap value from one range to another

unity_chain.description: Remap a value from one numeric range to another

steps.note: Add > Math > Remap

unity_chain.blender_conversion_notes: Direct mapping. Blender MapRange parameters: From Min/Max and To Min/Max map directly to Unity Remap node.

unity_chain.description: Additive blend of two shader contributions

steps.note: Add > Math > Add
Additive shaders may need Emission channel instead of Base Color to avoid PBR energy conservation clamping

unity_chain.blender_conversion_notes: Blender Add Shader adds two lighting contributions. In Unity, add the color values and output to Emission for additive glow effects.

unity_chain.description: Blend two shaders using a factor

steps.note: Add > Math > Lerp
If mixing transparent: also Lerp the Alpha values separately and connect to Alpha port

unity_chain.blender_conversion_notes: Blender Mix Shader blends two full shader trees. In Unity, Lerp the color outputs. For transparency mixing, also blend alpha values.

unity_chain.description: Convert color temperature (Kelvin) to RGB color

steps.note: Unity has no Blackbody node. Two options:

steps: Bake Blender Blackbody output across 0-10000K range to a 256x1 texture
Use Sample Texture 2D with Temperature/10000 as UV.x

steps.note: Use an approximation polynomial for blackbody. Create Custom Function node with HLSL blackbody approximation formula.
Common range: 1000K (red/orange) to 6500K (white) to 10000K (blue). Normalize input to 0-1 for LUT.

unity_chain.blender_conversion_notes: Bake the Blackbody node output across the relevant temperature range to a 1D LUT texture. Use temperature/maxTemp as UV lookup.

ShaderNodeColorRamp.description: Color gradient ramp (must bake to texture)

unity_chain.description: Map a value through a color gradient ramp

steps.note: Add > Utility > Sample Gradient
Right-click Sample Gradient > Edit Gradient to recreate the Blender ramp stops
Manually recreate each color stop. Blender uses Linear/Ease/B-Spline interpolation; match in Unity gradient inspector.

unity_chain.blender_conversion_notes: Open Blender ColorRamp and note all color stops and their positions. Recreate them in Unity's Sample Gradient node gradient editor.

unity_chain.description: Per-channel RGB curve adjustment (approximated with LUT or math chain)

steps.note: RGB curves are complex. Two approaches: LUT bake or math chain approximation.

steps: In Blender: screenshot or export the curve as a 1D LUT (gradient)
Bake curve output to a 1D texture strip
In Unity: use Sample Texture 2D with the LUT texture, driven by the input value
CREATE Split node (RGB -> R, G, B channels)
CREATE 3x Multiply nodes (one per channel with curve approximation weights)
CONNECT R -> Multiply_R, G -> Multiply_G, B -> Multiply_B

unity_chain.blender_conversion_notes: Blender RGB Curve is a non-linear per-channel adjustment. No direct Unity equivalent. LUT bake is most accurate; math chain is an approximation.

unity_chain.description: Single float curve (approximated)

steps.note: Float curve maps one value to another via a curve. Approximate with a baked gradient texture.
In Blender: plot the curve, export as 256x1 texture strip where X=input, Pixel value=output
Use a 256x1 LUT texture
If curve is simple (S-curve): approximate with Smoothstep node. For linear: use Remap/Map Range node.

unity_chain.blender_conversion_notes: Simple curves can use Smoothstep or Map Range. Complex curves must be baked to a 1D texture LUT.

unity_chain.description: Apply gamma correction to a color

steps.note: Add > Math > Power
Gamma value from Blender node
Unity also has a built-in Gamma node in some versions: Add > Artistic > Gamma (check availability in your ShaderGraph version)

unity_chain.blender_conversion_notes: Gamma correction = pow(color, gamma). Use Math > Power node if Gamma node is not available in your Unity ShaderGraph version.

unity_chain.description: Adjust brightness and contrast of a color

steps.note: For Contrast: Multiply node
For Brightness: Add node
Full formula: Output = (Input * Contrast) + Brightness

unity_chain.blender_conversion_notes: Unity has no direct Brightness/Contrast node. Approximate: (Color * Contrast) + Brightness using Multiply + Add.

unity_chain.description: Adjust hue, saturation and value of a color

steps.note: Unity ShaderGraph has no native HSV node. Requires custom function or approximate chain.
Add > Utility > Custom Function

steps.value: RGB to HSV conversion HLSL: float3 RGBtoHSV(float3 c){...} then shift H, scale S, scale V, convert back HSV to RGB

steps: For Saturation only: Use Lerp between grayscale and color. CREATE Dot(Color, float3(0.3,0.59,0.11)) for luma, then Lerp(luma, color, Saturation)
For Value only: Multiply color by value scalar

unity_chain.blender_conversion_notes: Full HSV requires a custom HLSL function in Unity. For saturation-only adjustments, use a Lerp between grey (luma) and the original color.

unity_chain.description: Ray tracing / path tracing info — NO real-time equivalent

steps.note: LightPath is a Cycles-only ray tracing node with no Unity ShaderGraph equivalent
Remove this node and its connected sub-graph from the converted shader
For Is Camera Ray: approximate with always-true (constant 1). For Is Shadow Ray: use Unity shadow receiving settings instead

unity_chain.blender_conversion_notes: LightPath outputs are only valid during Cycles path tracing. Skip this node in Unity conversion. Use Unity's material shadow settings instead.

unity_chain.description: Ambient Occlusion (must be pre-baked)

steps: In Blender: go to Render Properties > Bake
Set Bake Type to Ambient Occlusion
Bake to a new image texture
Import the baked AO texture into Unity
CONNECT Sample Texture 2D (output: R) -> Ambient Occlusion (Master node input)

unity_chain.blender_conversion_notes: AO cannot be calculated in real-time in Unity ShaderGraph. Must bake in Blender first, then use as a static texture input.

unity_chain.description: Particle system metadata — use Custom Vertex Attribute workaround

steps.note: Unity ShaderGraph has no Particle Info node equivalent

steps: If using Unity VFX Graph: expose particle attributes as custom vertex streams
Enable Custom Vertex Streams on Particle System Renderer
Use Vertex ID or Custom1/Custom2 streams in ShaderGraph

steps.note: For Age/Lifetime: pass via Custom Vertex Stream as TEXCOORD. For Index: use Vertex ID node (Add > Input > Vertex ID)

unity_chain.blender_conversion_notes: Blender particle metadata is not directly accessible in Unity ShaderGraph. Pass particle data via Custom Vertex Streams from Unity Particle System.

unity_chain.description: Hair system specific data — no equivalent

steps.note: Unity ShaderGraph has no Hair Info node
For Unity hair rendering: use HDRP Hair shader with built-in hair attributes
Hair shading in Unity HDRP uses dedicated Hair Master Node with Tangent, UV along strand, etc.

unity_chain.blender_conversion_notes: Hair info is Blender-specific. For Unity hair: use HDRP Hair Shader or a dedicated hair rendering plugin. Cannot be directly converted.

unity_chain.description: Compositor-only feature — NOT compatible

steps.note: Cryptomatte is a Blender compositor/render pass feature only. No shader equivalent exists.
Remove this node entirely from the shader graph. It has no role in real-time rendering.

unity_chain.blender_conversion_notes: Cryptomatte is only used for compositing passes in Blender. Cannot be converted. Remove from shader.

unity_chain.description: Volumetric absorption — Cycles only, NOT compatible with real-time

steps.note: Volume shaders are Cycles-specific and not supported in Unity ShaderGraph real-time rendering
For Unity volumetric fog effects: use Unity's Fog Volume component (HDRP) or Post Processing Fog
Remove this node from the shader. Replace with standard transparent surface if needed.

unity_chain.blender_conversion_notes: Volumetric shaders are not real-time compatible. Use Unity HDRP Fog Volumes for atmospheric effects instead.

unity_chain.description: Volumetric scattering — Cycles only, NOT compatible with real-time

steps.note: Volume scatter is Cycles path tracing only. No ShaderGraph equivalent.
For Unity: use HDRP Fog Volume with scattering coefficient, or VFX Graph for particle-based volume effects
Remove from shader. Handle volumetrics at scene level in Unity, not per-material.

unity_chain.blender_conversion_notes: Volumetric scattering has no real-time ShaderGraph equivalent. Use Unity HDRP Global/Local Fog Volumes at scene level.

unity_chain.description: Height-based displacement approximated as vertex offset

steps.note: True tessellation displacement requires HDRP with Tessellation enabled on the material

steps: Connect Height map to Tessellation Height input
CONNECT Normal From Height (Out) -> Multiply (A) | [Strength] -> Multiply (B)
CONNECT Position (Object Space) -> Add (A) | Multiply (Out) -> Add (B)

unity_chain.blender_conversion_notes: HDRP: use Tessellation + Height map. URP: approximate by offsetting Vertex Position along the normal using height * strength.

unity_chain.description: Per-vertex normal vector input

steps.note: Add > Input > Normal Vector
Match to Blender normal space

unity_chain.blender_conversion_notes: Blender Normal node outputs the vertex normal. Unity Normal Vector node does the same. Match the coordinate space (Object vs World).

unity_chain.description: Rotate a vector around an axis by an angle

steps.note: Add > Math > Rotate About Axis (or use Transform)

steps.from: [Angle in radians] -> Rotate About Axis (input: Rotation)

unity_chain.blender_conversion_notes: Blender VectorRotate supports multiple rotation types. Unity Rotate About Axis is the closest equivalent. Angles may need conversion (radians vs degrees).

unity_chain.description: Transform a vector between coordinate spaces

steps.note: Identify the From/To space in Blender: World, Object, or Camera (not supported in Unity)
Add > Math > Transform Direction
Match Blender Convert From space
Match Blender Convert To space

unity_chain.blender_conversion_notes: Match the coordinate space conversion. Camera space transforms have no Unity ShaderGraph equivalent and must be approximated.

unity_chain.description: Magic texture (sine-based color interference pattern)

steps.note: UV.x * frequency and UV.y * frequency
Sine(UV.x*freq) and Sine(UV.y*freq)
Sine_X + Sine_Y
Fraction to wrap to 0-1

steps.from: UV.x -> *freq -> Sine -> Add | UV.y -> *freq -> Cosine -> Add -> Fraction -> [output]

steps.note: Blender Scale maps to frequency

unity_chain.blender_conversion_notes: Magic texture uses sine wave interference. Scale controls frequency. Chain: Sine(UV.x*scale) + Cosine(UV.y*scale) -> Fraction for 0-1 output.

unity_chain.description: Stucci procedural texture (legacy Blender) — approximate with noise

steps.note: Stucci is a legacy Blender texture type (bump-like noise). Not available in Unity.

steps: This approximates Stucci's bumpy surface normal behavior

unity_chain.blender_conversion_notes: Stucci is used as a bump-noise. Approximate with Simple Noise → Normal From Height to get similar bumpy normal output.

unity_chain.description: Random white noise per UV cell

steps.note: Unity Simple Noise at very high scale approximates white noise
High scale = high frequency noise approaching white noise
For per-pixel random: CREATE Custom Function node with hash function: frac(sin(dot(UV, float2(12.9898, 78.233))) * 43758.5453)

unity_chain.blender_conversion_notes: White noise = per-pixel random value. Best approximated with HLSL hash function in Custom Function node: frac(sin(dot(uv, float2(12.9898,78.233)))*43758.5453).

unity_chain.description: Point density texture — must be pre-baked

steps: In Blender: Bake the Point Density texture to an image texture (Render > Bake, type: Emit)

unity_chain.blender_conversion_notes: Point Density is baked from particle/point cloud data. Pre-bake in Blender, then use as static texture in Unity.

unity_chain.description: IES light profile (must be pre-baked to texture)

steps.note: Render IES lighting contribution to a lightmap texture in Blender

unity_chain.blender_conversion_notes: IES profiles define light distribution. Pre-bake the IES lighting to a lightmap in Blender and sample it in Unity.

unity_chain.description: Refraction shader approximated with transparent + glossy combination

steps.note: Blend Transparent and Glossy
1 - Fresnel for Transparent weight
High smoothness for glass-like refraction look
True refraction (bending light) is not supported in Unity URP ShaderGraph. Use grab pass texture in URP for screen-space refraction.

unity_chain.blender_conversion_notes: True refraction requires a grab pass / screen-space refraction technique. Approximate with Fresnel-blended transparency + high smoothness.

unity_chain.description: Backlit translucent material (light passes through)

steps.note: Use a Diffuse node wired to Base Color
Add > Math > Add
Set alpha from [Fac] input
True subsurface translucency requires Unity HDRP Lit node with Subsurface Scattering

unity_chain.blender_conversion_notes: Blender translucent = light from behind passes through. Approximate in Unity by additive diffuse + partial transparency.

unity_chain.description: Fabric-like sheen/velvet edge glow (complex approximation)

steps.note: Add > Input > Fresnel
Multiply Fresnel by Sheen Color
For HDRP: use Fabric shader with Sheen parameters directly

unity_chain.blender_conversion_notes: Blender Sheen is a fabric BRDF lobe. Unity URP has no direct equivalent; approximate with Fresnel-multiplied rim color added to diffuse.

unity_chain.description: Brushed metal / anisotropic highlight shader

steps.note: True anisotropy requires HDRP Lit Node with Anisotropy parameter enabled
In HDRP: Add Lit Master > enable Anisotropy > connect Tangent and Anisotropy inputs directly

unity_chain.blender_conversion_notes: Anisotropy requires either HDRP's Lit node or a custom HLSL approach. In URP, approximate with Tangent-modulated roughness.

unity_chain.description: Cel-shaded / toon ramp shader

steps.note: Add > Math > Step (quantizes smooth gradient to bands)
Adjust for hard vs soft toon line
Add > Utility > Sample Gradient (for color banding)

steps.from: Step (Out) -> Sample Gradient (input: Time) -> Base Color

unity_chain.blender_conversion_notes: Toon shader needs quantized lighting. Use Step to create hard bands, then Sample Gradient to map bands to colors.

unity_chain.description: Skin / wax translucency approximation

steps.note: Best result: use HDRP Lit node with Subsurface Scattering enabled

steps: Set Material Type to Subsurface Scattering
Assign Diffusion Profile matching Blender SSS Radius values

steps.note: In URP (no native SSS): approximate with Diffuse + Translucent addition

unity_chain.blender_conversion_notes: SSS requires HDRP for proper support. Export Blender SSS thickness map (baked) and use as HDRP Thickness input.

unity_chain.description: Holdout (compositor matte) — NOT compatible

steps.note: Holdout is a Blender compositor feature for punching holes in the render. No Unity ShaderGraph equivalent.
Remove this node. If transparency/cutout is needed, use Alpha Clip on the material instead.

unity_chain.blender_conversion_notes: Holdout has no Unity equivalent. Use Alpha Clipping or Stencil buffer techniques for similar masking effects in Unity.

unity_chain.description: Light falloff control — rendering pipeline only, NOT compatible

steps.note: Light Falloff controls per-shader light distance attenuation in Cycles. Real-time renderers handle this automatically.
Remove this node. In Unity, light falloff is controlled by the Light component (Range, Intensity) not per-material shaders.

unity_chain.blender_conversion_notes: Light falloff is Cycles-specific. In Unity, adjust the Light component's Range and Intensity. Cannot be done in ShaderGraph.

unity_chain.description: Instance array metadata — NOT compatible

steps.note: Array Info provides Blender geometry instance data. No direct Unity ShaderGraph equivalent.
For Unity GPU instancing: use UNITY_INSTANCE_ID and instanced property blocks. Requires custom HLSL.
Access UNITY_MATRIX_M or per-instance data via Custom Function HLSL block

unity_chain.blender_conversion_notes: Geometry instance data requires custom HLSL in Unity. Use GPU Instancing with instanced properties for per-instance variation.

unity_chain.description: Bevel effect to soften hard edges

steps.note: Unity ShaderGraph does not have a native Bevel node. Two approaches:
Use Normal Detail node to add fine edge normal detail from a bevel normal map baked in Blender

steps: In Blender: bake the Bevel node output to a Normal map texture

steps.note: Use the Unity Bevel node if available (HDRP only): Add > Input > Bevel

unity_chain.blender_conversion_notes: Bake Blender's Bevel node output as a normal map. Import into Unity as Normal type and apply via Normal From Texture node.

ShaderNodeShaderToRGB.description: Eevee-only Shader to RGB (not supported in Unity)

unity_chain.description: Eevee Shader to RGB — NOT supported in Unity

steps.note: Shader to RGB is an Eevee-exclusive node that captures the rendered color of a shader. No Unity equivalent.
For toon/post-process effects: use Unity Post Processing Stack (Outline, Color Grading) instead
Remove this node and its downstream sub-graph. Rebuild the effect using Unity post-processing.

unity_chain.blender_conversion_notes: Shader To RGB is Eevee-only and cannot be converted. Rebuild downstream effects using Unity's post-processing stack.

ShaderNodeScript.description: OSL script node (not supported in Unity)

unity_chain.description: OSL Script node — NOT supported in Unity

steps.note: OSL (Open Shading Language) scripts are Cycles-only and not compatible with HLSL/Unity ShaderGraph.
Port the OSL logic to HLSL manually and use a Custom Function node in Unity ShaderGraph
Add > Utility > Custom Function — paste translated HLSL code

unity_chain.blender_conversion_notes: OSL is Cycles-specific. Manually translate the OSL shader code to HLSL and use a Unity Custom Function node.

unity_chain.description: Procedural sky texture — approximate with gradient or HDR cubemap

steps.note: Blender Sky Texture generates a physically-based sky model. Unity has no direct equivalent in ShaderGraph.

steps: In Blender: set up an HDRI sky environment
Import into Unity as Cubemap (Texture Shape: Cube)
Use Sample Cubemap node in ShaderGraph
CREATE Split (to get Y for vertical gradient)
CREATE Sample Gradient (horizon color to zenith color)
CONNECT UV.y -> Sample Gradient -> Base Color

unity_chain.blender_conversion_notes: Export Blender sky as .hdr panorama. Import to Unity as Cubemap. Use Sample Cubemap node for environment-matched sky color sampling.

unity_chain.description: Volume shader info (Cycles only) — NOT compatible

steps.note: Volume Info provides data inside volumetric shaders which are Cycles-only.
Remove this node. There is no Unity ShaderGraph equivalent for volumetric density/color/flame/temperature data.

unity_chain.blender_conversion_notes: Volume Info is Cycles-only. Cannot be converted to Unity ShaderGraph. Remove from shader.

unity_chain.description: Geometry input data (position, normal, UV, tangent, etc.)

steps.note: Blender NewGeometry outputs multiple values. Map each used output separately:
Add > Input > Position (World or Object space)
Add > Input > Normal Vector
Add > Input > Tangent Vector
Use Object space Normal Vector
Add > Input > View Direction
Maps to UV channel 0

unity_chain.blender_conversion_notes: NewGeometry is a multi-output node. Check which outputs are actually connected in the Blender graph and only create the corresponding Unity nodes.

unity_chain.description: Object metadata (location, random ID, etc.)

steps.note: Blender ObjectInfo outputs: Location, Color, Alpha, Object Index, Material Index, Random
CREATE Custom Function: float random = frac(sin(dot(UNITY_MATRIX_M[3].xyz, float3(12.9898, 78.233, 45.164))) * 43758.5453)
No dynamic equivalent in Unity; hardcode or use Material Property Block

unity_chain.blender_conversion_notes: ObjectInfo is Blender-specific. Location maps to Object Position. Random can be approximated with a hash of the object world matrix.

unity_chain.description: Camera data — NOT directly available in Unity ShaderGraph

steps.note: Unity ShaderGraph has no Camera Data node equivalent
For View Distance approximation: CREATE View Direction node (Add > Input > View Direction)
For camera depth: CREATE Scene Depth node (Add > Input > Scene Depth) in URP/HDRP

unity_chain.blender_conversion_notes: Camera Data is Blender/Cycles specific. Map View Distance to Unity Scene Depth. Orthographic Scale has no Unity ShaderGraph equivalent.

unity_chain.description: Velvet / fabric surface with edge sheen

steps.node: Diffuse base (connect Color to Base Color)

steps.note: For edge glow effect
Fresnel * Velvet Color

steps.from: Diffuse + Multiply (edge glow) -> Add -> Base Color

steps.note: No specular for velvet

unity_chain.blender_conversion_notes: Velvet = soft diffuse + Fresnel edge rim. Set Smoothness to 0. The Fresnel edge lit with the velvet color gives the characteristic fabric look.

ShaderNodeSeparateHSV.description: Separate color into HSV components (requires custom function)

ShaderNodeCombineHSV.description: Combine HSV components into color (requires custom function)
